div.h2: read_request() Accumulation Loop — Step-by-Step Buffer State

div.p: Legend 🟣 Header segment · 🔵 Data bytes 🟩 Free / available · ⬜ Unwritten 🔴 Error / abort path

g.text: Edge Case C — Buffer Exhausted (overflow guard)

div.p: Invariant: buf[total] = '\0' → buf[0] = '\0' Guard: total < buf_size - 1 → 0 < 8191 ✓

buf[ 0 .. 311 ] 🔵 312 bytes from network GET /index.html HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:8080
 User-Agent: curl/7.88.1
Accept: */*
 Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
 ← truncated mid-header buf[312] = '\0' ← null-terminator written buf[ 312 .. 8191 ] ░░ 7880 bytes still free

buf[ 0 .. 311 ] 🔵 from recv call #1 (unchanged) buf[ 312 .. 400 ] 🟣 89 new bytes: Cache-Control: max-age=0
 ← 26 bytes Connection: keep-alive
 ← 24 bytes 
 ← END-OF-HEADERS at offset 398..401 buf[401] = '\0' ← null-terminator written buf[ 401 .. 8191 ] ░░ 7791 bytes still free

Pointer arithmetic detail: Pass 1: recv(fd, buf+0, 8191) → wrote buf[0..311] Pass 2: recv(fd, buf+312, 7879) → wrote buf[312..400] buf[401] = '\0' (null-terminator) strstr scans buf[0..400], finds \r\n\r\n at offset 398
When: client performs TCP orderly shutdown (FIN) before sending \r\n\r\n n = recv(fd, buf + total, buf_size - 1 - total, 0) → n = 0
Caller in handle_client() logs "read_request failed or client disconnected" and returns without sending a response. close(client_fd) by caller.
When: network error, ECONNRESET, or signal (errno == EINTR) n = recv(fd, buf + total, ..., 0) → n = -1
When: client sends headers > 8191 bytes with no \r\n\r\n Top of loop checks: total < buf_size - 1 → 8191 >= 8191: condition FALSE → loop exits
Caller sends 400 Bad Request (or 431 Request Header Fields Too Large). Prevents unbounded accumulation / heap exhaustion attack.

Guard invariant on every recv() call: recv(fd, buf + total, buf_size - 1 - total, 0) The third argument is always ≥ 1 when the loop condition holds, so recv() is never called with length 0. After recv(): buf[total] = '\0' uses buf[total] which is always within buf[0..buf_size-1] because total ≤ buf_size - 1.

tr.td: write cursor — advances by n each call

div.p: Invariant holding at every strstr() call: buf[0..total-1] = accumulated bytes from all recv() calls buf[total] = '\0' → strstr() cannot overrun total < buf_size → buf[total] is always in-bounds
